mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening corporate output capacity, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns specifically during late-cycle phases, or the predictive alpha vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings surpassing 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or fails to outperform a baseline momentum strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP revisions below trend signal weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during early recession phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines do not predict negative 20-day returns during early recession periods, or the signal predictive power is fully subsumed by equity risk premium movements.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly changes show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
